headline: Amazon Hit by Federal Labour Investigation: Widespread Wage Theft and Safety Violations across 23 Distribution Hubs.
summary: The US Department of Labor opens a formal judicial investigation into Amazon.com Inc. following structural evidence of illegal overtime practices and unreported warehouse safety violations.

headline: Oregon Ducks Hosting Major Notre Dame Recruiting Target on Campus
summary:
According to Rivals, Parks visited both Notre Dame and Oregon in April before returning to both schools for official visits in June. The Ducks hold the advantage of hosting Parks for his last visit, and Oregon could be trying to sell the four-star defensive lineman on joining a talented group of recruits in the Ducks' class of 2027.

headline: Texas A&M Knows Its Next Defensive Target After Losing on John Meredith III
summary:
The No. 2 recruit in the country, John Meredith III, announced his commitment to the Texas Longhorns on Friday, June 19, after a long and competitive process. The Texas A&M Aggies were among the finalists until the very end before losing to their rivals.

Certainly, the Aggies will not be celebrating missing out on the nation's top cornerback. Howe

headline: Poll: Americans believe they aren't being taught enough about democracy
summary:
For Thomas Jefferson, James Madison and other bold-face names struggling to bring about a new nation, an educated and informed citizenry was essential to the success of the American experiment.

headline: Anthropic, and the Myth of AI Sovereignty
summary:
The contest over AI is creating a duopolistic world in which technological power, rather than territory, defines geopolitical influence.

If the preceding Industrial Revolutions went hand in hand with the nation-state, the ongoing Fourth Industrial Revolution appears to bypass the state altogether.
